summary: The test suite provides solid coverage of core validator functions (classify, report generation, formatting) and includes integration tests via act. However, notable gaps exist: CLI argument parsing (parseArgs) is untested, error cases in integration tests are absent (invalid fixtures, malformed JSON, empty arrays), and only 2 fixture cases are tested when a warning-only case should also be validated. Unit tests use good boundary conditions (exact expiry date) but miss edge cases (very old dates, special characters, large datasets). Design is clean with clear test organization, deterministic fixtures (REF_NOW), and good separation of concerns; act integration tests properly validate end-to-end behavior through actual GitHub Actions execution. The suite would catch regressions in core logic but would miss CLI-layer errors and several edge cases.
